prerequisites: A self-hosted or WordPress.com site reachable over HTTPS at a known domain (provided in the `domain` config field).
WordPress 5.6 or later, which ships the REST API and Application Passwords by default.
A WordPress user account whose username is supplied in the `username` field and which has capabilities to read the content you want to sync.
An Application Password generated for that user account (used as the `password` field). The site must be served over HTTPS for Application Password authentication to be accepted.
A `start_date` for the earliest records to sync, and optionally a `lookback_window` to re-fetch recently updated records.

authMethods.name: Basic authentication with WordPress username and Application Password
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates against the WordPress REST API using HTTP Basic Authentication. Supply your WordPress account `username` and a `password` value. As of WordPress 5.6, the recommended password is an Application Password generated from the user's profile, which is sent with each request using standard Basic Auth (RFC 7617) over HTTPS. These map directly to the connector's `username` and `password` secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Confirm REST API access on your WordPress site
setupSteps.body: Ensure your WordPress site runs version 5.6 or later and is reachable over HTTPS. The REST API ships enabled by default and exchanges data as JSON objects. Note the site domain you will sync from; you will provide it in the `domain` config field.

setupSteps.title: Generate an Application Password
setupSteps.body: Sign in to wp-admin as the user you want the connector to act as. Go to Users and edit that user (wp-admin -> Users -> Edit User), then create a new Application Password under the Application Passwords section. Copy the generated password value immediately, since WordPress shows it only once. Application Passwords are tied to the individual user account and are passed via Basic Authentication over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your WordPress `username` and the generated Application Password as the `password` field. Set `domain` to your site's domain and set `start_date` to the earliest date to sync. Optionally set `lookback_window` to re-read records updated within a recent window.
